title: ConcatTreesHelper::Empty buffer
description: Helper freshly created or with no trees pushed; building yields an empty TokenStream and appending is a no-op.

evidence.explanation:
**Entity:** ConcatTreesHelper

**State:** Empty

**State invariants:**
- self.trees.is_empty() == true
- build(self) returns TokenStream(None)
- append_to(self, _) returns early without mutating the target stream

**Transitions:**
- Empty -> NonEmpty via push(...)
- Empty -> Consumed via build(self)
- Empty -> Consumed via append_to(self, _)

**Evidence:** line 17-19: new() constructs ConcatTreesHelper with an empty Vec (Vec::with_capacity); line 26: branch explicitly checks self.trees.is_empty(); line 27: empty case constructs TokenStream(None); line 34-35: append_to returns early if self.trees.is_empty()

**Implementation:** Define ConcatTreesHelper<S> with zero-sized states Empty/NonEmpty. new() -> ConcatTreesHelper<Empty>. push(self or &mut self) -> ConcatTreesHelper<NonEmpty>. Provide build(self) and append_to(self, &mut TokenStream) only for ConcatTreesHelper<NonEmpty> so empty helpers cannot be built/appended at compile time.

title: ConcatTreesHelper::NonEmpty buffer
description: Helper has at least one tree; building yields a non-empty TokenStream and appending mutates the target stream.

evidence.explanation:
**Entity:** ConcatTreesHelper

**State:** NonEmpty

**State invariants:**
- self.trees.is_empty() == false
- build(self) constructs TokenStream(Some(...))
- append_to(self, stream) assigns stream.0 = Some(ts_concat_trees(...))

**Transitions:**
- NonEmpty -> Consumed via build(self)
- NonEmpty -> Consumed via append_to(self, _)

**Evidence:** line 21-23: push() inserts a tree into self.trees; line 29: non-empty case constructs TokenStream(Some(BridgeMethods::ts_concat_trees(...))); line 37: append_to sets stream.0 = Some(BridgeMethods::ts_concat_trees(stream.0.take(), self.trees))

**Implementation:** Expose build(self) and append_to(self, &mut TokenStream) only for ConcatTreesHelper<NonEmpty>, eliminating runtime emptiness checks and guaranteeing non-empty concatenations at compile time.

title: TokenStream::Empty
description: Represents an empty token stream encoded as an internal None; concatenation with a non-empty helper transitions it to Some(..).

evidence.explanation:
**Entity:** TokenStream

**State:** Empty

**State invariants:**
- internal representation is None (empty)
- ts_concat_trees is not invoked when producing an empty stream

**Transitions:**
- Empty -> NonEmpty via ConcatTreesHelper::build(non-empty)
- Empty -> NonEmpty via ConcatTreesHelper::append_to(non-empty)

**Evidence:** line 27: TokenStream(None) is returned when helper is empty; line 29: contrast shows non-empty is represented as TokenStream(Some(...)); line 37: append_to assigns stream.0 = Some(...) regardless of prior value; if previously None, becomes non-empty

**Implementation:** Model TokenStream<S> with states Empty/NonEmpty. Provide operations that require content (e.g., concatenation that assumes non-empty) only on TokenStream<NonEmpty>. Conversions from Empty to NonEmpty occur via building/appending with a NonEmpty helper.

title: TokenStream::NonEmpty
description: Represents a non-empty token stream encoded as an internal Some(..); further concatenations keep it non-empty.

evidence.explanation:
**Entity:** TokenStream

**State:** NonEmpty

**State invariants:**
- internal representation is Some(..) (non-empty)
- concatenation uses BridgeMethods::ts_concat_trees(stream.0.take(), ...)

**Transitions:**
- NonEmpty -> NonEmpty via ConcatTreesHelper::append_to(non-empty)

**Evidence:** line 29: TokenStream(Some(...)) is constructed when helper is non-empty; line 37: append_to uses stream.0.take() and assigns Some(...), preserving non-empty state

**Implementation:** Introduce TokenStream<NonEmpty> to represent streams guaranteed to have content; methods that rely on non-emptiness are only implemented for this state, eliminating need for Option-based checks at call sites.

title: TokenStream::Empty state
description: Represents a token stream with no underlying bridge::client::TokenStream (internal Option is None).

evidence.explanation:
**Entity:** TokenStream

**State:** Empty

**State invariants:**
- self.0 == None (no underlying stream present)
- append_to with a helper that has exactly one element assigns that single element directly
- building from an empty helper yields TokenStream(None)

**Transitions:**
- Empty -> NonEmpty via ConcatStreamsHelper::append_to when helper has >= 1 element (lines 31-35)
- Empty -> Empty via ConcatStreamsHelper::build when helper is empty (lines 19-21)

**Evidence:** line 30: let base = stream.0.take(); // base is Option, can be None; line 31: if base.is_none() && self.streams.len() == 1 { ... } // special-case when TokenStream is Empty; line 32: stream.0 = self.streams.pop(); // assigns the single element directly when base was None; line 20: TokenStream(self.streams.pop()) // pop on empty helper returns None, producing an Empty TokenStream

**Implementation:** Model TokenStream<S> with states Empty/NonEmpty using PhantomData. Provide concat/append only on TokenStream<Empty> -> TokenStream<NonEmpty> or TokenStream<NonEmpty> -> TokenStream<NonEmpty>, eliminating runtime is_none checks and making Empty vs NonEmpty a compile-time property.

title: TokenStream::NonEmpty state
description: Represents a token stream that wraps a valid bridge::client::TokenStream (internal Option is Some).

evidence.explanation:
**Entity:** TokenStream

**State:** NonEmpty

**State invariants:**
- self.0 == Some(bridge::client::TokenStream)
- append_to produces Some(...) by concatenation when base was Some
- helper::push only ever extracts and forwards Some(...) variants into its storage

**Transitions:**
- NonEmpty -> NonEmpty via ConcatStreamsHelper::append_to (concatenation path, line 34)

**Evidence:** line 22: TokenStream(Some(BridgeMethods::ts_concat_streams(None, self.streams))) // explicit construction of NonEmpty; line 34: stream.0 = Some(BridgeMethods::ts_concat_streams(base, self.streams)); // stays NonEmpty after append; line 13: if let Some(stream) = stream.0 { self.streams.push(stream); } // only Some(..) forwarded

**Implementation:** Expose TokenStream<NonEmpty> as a distinct state; methods that require an underlying stream (e.g., concatenation producing guaranteed output) operate only on NonEmpty, avoiding repeated Option checks.

title: ConcatStreamsHelper::Empty accumulator
description: Helper has accumulated no non-empty streams.

evidence.explanation:
**Entity:** ConcatStreamsHelper

**State:** Empty

**State invariants:**
- self.streams.is_empty() == true
- append_to is a no-op in this state
- build() returns TokenStream(None)

**Transitions:**
- Empty -> NonEmpty via push(non-empty TokenStream) (lines 12-15)

**Evidence:** line 27: if self.streams.is_empty() { return; } // append_to is a no-op when empty; line 20: TokenStream(self.streams.pop()) // pop on empty returns None, yielding an empty TokenStream; line 13: if let Some(stream) = stream.0 { self.streams.push(stream); } // only non-empty pushes change the state

**Implementation:** Use a builder typestate: ConcatStreamsHelper<Empty> returned by new(); push(TokenStream<NonEmpty>) -> ConcatStreamsHelper<NonEmpty>. Provide build()/append_to only for ConcatStreamsHelper<NonEmpty> to avoid empty-case branches; optionally a separate build_empty() for the Empty state.

title: ConcatStreamsHelper::NonEmpty accumulator
description: Helper has accumulated at least one non-empty underlying stream; all stored elements are guaranteed non-empty.

evidence.explanation:
**Entity:** ConcatStreamsHelper

**State:** NonEmpty

**State invariants:**
- self.streams.len() >= 1
- self.streams contains only non-empty bridge::client::TokenStream values (empties are filtered out in push)
- When len == 1 and base TokenStream is Empty, append_to can assign the single element directly without concatenation

**Transitions:**
- NonEmpty -> NonEmpty via additional push(non-empty TokenStream) (lines 12-15)
- NonEmpty -> (consumed) via build() or append_to() (lines 18-24, 26-36)

**Evidence:** line 13: if let Some(stream) = stream.0 { self.streams.push(stream); } // establishes 'only non-empty' invariant; line 19: if self.streams.len() <= 1 { ... } else { ... } // behavior depends on having accumulated one vs multiple; line 31: if base.is_none() && self.streams.len() == 1 { ... } // relies on 'only non-empty' to directly move the single element; line 32: stream.0 = self.streams.pop(); // safe to move because the sole element is known to be non-empty

**Implementation:** Encode cardinality at the type level: ConcatStreamsHelper<NonEmpty> (at least one) vs <Empty>. Specialize build/append_to for <NonEmpty> to remove is_empty and Some/None branching; internally store a NonEmptyStream newtype to reflect the 'only non-empty' invariant.

title: __rust_panic_cleanup::Unreachable in abort runtime
description: In the panic=abort runtime, this cleanup entry point must never be invoked; reaching it is a bug.

evidence.explanation:
**Entity:** __rust_panic_cleanup

**State:** Unreachable

**State invariants:**
- No unwinding path exists in panic=abort, so no payload cleanup is ever required
- Calling this function will unconditionally panic via unreachable!()

**Evidence:** line 27: pub unsafe extern "C" fn __rust_panic_cleanup(...; line 28: unreachable!() — enforces that this code path is never valid at runtime; line 77: comment: "it should never be called as we don't link in an unwinding runtime at all."

**Implementation:** Gate the availability of a cleanup function behind a sealed Unwind capability/token; only the unwinding runtime constructs this token. In panic=abort builds, the token is unnameable so any cleanup entry point is not callable, making misuse a compile error.

title: __rust_start_panic::Abort-only behavior
description: On panic, optionally attach a platform-specific message and then abort the process; this function never returns.

evidence.explanation:
**Entity:** __rust_start_panic

**State:** AbortImmediately

**State invariants:**
- The panic payload is intentionally leaked (not cleaned up)
- If available, platform hooks attach an abort message
- __rust_abort is called and never returns, so the function does not return to the caller

**Evidence:** line 31: comment: "Leak" the payload and shim to the relevant abort; line 35: #[cfg(target_os = "android")] hook to set abort message; line 39: #[cfg(target_os = "zkvm")] hook to set abort message; line 47: extern declaration: safe fn __rust_abort() -> !; — diverging; line 50: __rust_abort() — guarantees non-return

**Implementation:** Introduce an internal abort_with_payload(_: &mut dyn PanicPayload) -> ! wrapper and restrict all internal call sites to the diverging API. Keep the symbol-matching __rust_start_panic as a thin, unreachable-after-call shim to preserve ABI, but make internal code use the !-returning newtyped API so the non-returning invariant is enforced at compile time.

title: rust_eh_catch_typeinfo::Unused in abort runtime
description: Provided only for linkage, this typeinfo must never be used when panic=abort because no exceptions are generated and foreign exceptions are UB.

evidence.explanation:
**Entity:** personalities::rust_eh_catch_typeinfo

**State:** Unused

**State invariants:**
- No catch_unwind path will consult this typeinfo in panic=abort
- Presence is for satisfying linker/ABI expectations, not for runtime use

**Evidence:** line 84: comment: corresponds to `eh_catch_typeinfo`; line 87: comment: panics don't generate exceptions with panic=abort; line 89: comment: catch_unwind calls will never use this typeinfo; line 93: static rust_eh_catch_typeinfo: [usize; 2] = [0; 2]; — inert placeholder

**Implementation:** Expose access to any EH typeinfo behind an Unwind capability so that in panic=abort configurations the capability is unnameable; this prevents any code from referencing the symbol at compile time.
